raw:
```json
{
  "criterion": "architecture",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "Backend layering is clean: policy/index.ts holds all rules and pure functions (MAX_ACTIVE_LOANS, LOAN_PERIOD_DAYS, computeFineCents, canBorrow, nextReservationToNotify), and services import from policy rather than duplicating constants. Domain errors are a small named hierarchy (ConflictError, ForbiddenError, NotFoundError, PaymentRequiredError in errors.ts) used consistently across services. madge.json shows no cycles and no orphans. File sizes are healthy: largest file is app.ts at 173 lines; nothing exceeds 200 lines, far from god-file territory. The frontend has a sensible pages/components/api.ts/types.ts split. However, the candidate falls short of level 4 on three counts: (1) services call raw db.prepare(...).run/get inline (loan-service.ts:41-47, 58, 71-73, 86-90) rather than going through a thin typed DB-access module, leaking SQL into the business layer; (2) the frontend is organized by technical role (pages/, components/) rather than by feature (no checkout/, members/, books/ directories owning their own components/hooks/api calls); (3) FE/BE types are not shared from a single source — frontend/src/types.ts (58 lines) coexists with backend/src/services/types.ts (65 lines) with no shared workspace or generated client. Also, the backend tree shows an empty routes/ directory while HTTP handlers presumably live inside app.ts, which is a minor inconsistency rather than a leak. Overall: clean enough, not yet well-modularized."
}
```

parsed.rationale: Backend layering is clean: policy/index.ts holds all rules and pure functions (MAX_ACTIVE_LOANS, LOAN_PERIOD_DAYS, computeFineCents, canBorrow, nextReservationToNotify), and services import from policy rather than duplicating constants. Domain errors are a small named hierarchy (ConflictError, ForbiddenError, NotFoundError, PaymentRequiredError in errors.ts) used consistently across services. madge.json shows no cycles and no orphans. File sizes are healthy: largest file is app.ts at 173 lines; nothing exceeds 200 lines, far from god-file territory. The frontend has a sensible pages/components/api.ts/types.ts split. However, the candidate falls short of level 4 on three counts: (1) services call raw db.prepare(...).run/get inline (loan-service.ts:41-47, 58, 71-73, 86-90) rather than going through a thin typed DB-access module, leaking SQL into the business layer; (2) the frontend is organized by technical role (pages/, components/) rather than by feature (no checkout/, members/, books/ directories owning their own components/hooks/api calls); (3) FE/BE types are not shared from a single source — frontend/src/types.ts (58 lines) coexists with backend/src/services/types.ts (65 lines) with no shared workspace or generated client. Also, the backend tree shows an empty routes/ directory while HTTP handlers presumably live inside app.ts, which is a minor inconsistency rather than a leak. Overall: clean enough, not yet well-modularized.
